accept: Design decision documented (convention, new act surface, or other) in orchestration-design.md
If a new act CLI surface is needed (e.g. 'act show --branch'), file a separate implementation issue and link from this issue
Global act skill is updated to reference the chosen mechanism so workers and orchestrators agree
payload.description:
The push-asymmetry framing in orchestration-design.md has workers pushing to "their assigned branch" and the orchestrator merging that branch to main. There is currently no act surface for the orchestrator to learn which branch belongs to which issue. The Claude-Code-with-sub-agents reference implementation works today only because the parent session creates the worktree itself and knows the branch name out-of-band; any external orchestrator (gas town on beads, or any future harness) without that out-of-band knowledge has no path.

Design options to choose from:
(a) Convention — `worktree-<issue-id>` baked into the skill and act tooling so orchestrators can construct the branch name from the issue id.
(b) Structured field — `act show <id> --branch` returns a branch ref pulled from a field on the claim op (worker writes its branch when claiming).
(c) Note-grep — worker writes a note on claim ("worktree=worktree-abc1234") and orchestrator greps notes to discover.

Each has trade-offs (convention is rigid; structured field needs a small claim-op schema addition; note-grep is loose). Pick one before the first non-CC orchestrator runs in earnest, so workers and orchestrators agree on the contract.

Surfaced in the act-334e review (orchestration-design abstractions reviewer, 90% confidence; implementation feasibility reviewer corroborated).
payload.title: Specify orchestrator's branch-discovery surface — how an orchestrator learns which branch is working a given issue
